name: SafetyDocs360 My AI Engine Clean Start
purpose: Portable starter data for building a fresh SafetyDocs360 AI Engine around the proven deterministic safety-risk core.

keep: Deterministic 0-100 safety risk scoring before any LLM call.
Risk bands: low, moderate, high, critical.
Transparent top drivers, missing information, and next action output.
Critical/high risk escalation and human review gates.
Conservative handling when data is missing or source coverage is weak.
Gus guardrails: draft-only, no approval, no invented regulations, no compliance guarantees.
Domain understanding rules for common high-risk construction work.
Control recommendation rules for hot work, excavation, falls, electrical/LOTO, mobile equipment, and weather.
leaveBehindForFreshStart: Current UI shell and dashboard layout.
Authentication, roles, billing, deployment settings, and environment variables.
Database migrations and live customer tables.
Super Admin operational screens until the new engine contracts are stable.
Any LLM-first decision path that is not backed by deterministic rules.
Any feature that claims approval, release for work, or guaranteed compliance.

mission.product: SafetyDocs360 helps companies prevent incidents, predict safety risk, manage compliance documentation, and improve safety performance.
enginePrinciples: Human life and serious injury prevention come before productivity, cost, or schedule.
Compliance is the floor, not the ceiling.
The engine must be transparent and explainable.
When data is missing, be conservative and show the missing information.
High and critical risks must be escalated.
Critical risks should recommend immediate review and possible stop-work evaluation.
The AI should assist safety professionals, not replace them.
Do not invent regulatory citations or claim guaranteed compliance.
Use practical safety controls and prioritize the hierarchy of controls.

riskLevels.defaultAction: Continue monitoring and documenting field conditions.
Review risk controls during the current shift.
Verify controls before affected work continues.
Pause affected work for human review.

scoreInputs.scale: Each input is normalized to 1-5, then converted to a 0-100 score.
scoreInputs.severity: Fatal, catastrophic, critical, urgent, high, serious, moderate, medium, low, minor, or explicit 1-5 value.
scoreInputs.likelihood: Open risk signals, repeated items, severe signals, incident/near-miss pressure, convergence across sources.
scoreInputs.exposureFrequency: Crew exposure, scheduled high-risk work, explicit exposure signal.
scoreInputs.controlGap: Missing, ineffective, partial, effective, or unknown controls plus critical-control gaps.
scoreInputs.dataConfidenceConcern: Missing information, weak source coverage, or low data completeness.

escalationOverrides.reason: Imminent-danger indicators should not be downgraded by low numeric inputs.
Fatal or catastrophic severity paired with weak controls requires immediate review.

escalationOverrides.trigger: high-consequence hazard with critical controls not verified
escalationOverrides.result: at least high, critical when severity is 5
escalationOverrides.reason: Critical controls must be positively verified before treating high-consequence work as controlled.

escalationOverrides.trigger: high-risk task with missing training, permit, competent-person review, or overdue corrective action
escalationOverrides.result: increase risk level by one band
escalationOverrides.reason: Readiness gaps on high-risk work require conservative escalation.

confidenceRules.rule: At least four source categories, no missing data, and data completeness at least 0.8.
At least two signals and no more than three missing-data items.
Data-confidence concern is 5, four or more missing-data items, no recognized concepts, or weak source coverage.

SafetyAiInput.controlEffectiveness: missing | ineffective | partial | effective | unknown | null
SafetyAiInput.scores: optional explicit severity, likelihood, exposureFrequency, controlGap, dataConfidenceConcern values

SafetyAiScoreExplanation.dataInputs: short list of source signals used

controlRecommendationRules.title: Verify hot work permit and fire controls
controlRecommendationRules.recommendedAction: Require or verify an active hot work permit, fire watch, extinguisher, and combustible-material control before grinding, welding, cutting, or spark-producing work starts.
controlRecommendationRules.verificationRequired: Confirm permit status, fire watch assignment, extinguisher location, and combustible clearance at the work face.
controlRecommendationRules.humanApprovalReason: Hot work requires human permit and fire-control verification before work proceeds.

controlRecommendationRules.title: Verify competent-person excavation inspection
controlRecommendationRules.recommendedAction: Verify competent-person inspection, protective system, access/egress, spoil placement, and changing-condition review before trench or excavation entry.
controlRecommendationRules.verificationRequired: Document competent-person inspection and protective-system readiness before entry.
controlRecommendationRules.humanApprovalReason: Excavation or trench entry needs competent-person verification before workers enter.

controlRecommendationRules.title: Review fall protection and rescue readiness
controlRecommendationRules.recommendedAction: Review the fall protection plan, anchor/tie-off method, rescue readiness, and access controls before elevated work starts.
controlRecommendationRules.verificationRequired: Verify guardrail, anchor, tie-off, scaffold/MEWP inspection, and rescue plan readiness at the work area.
controlRecommendationRules.humanApprovalReason: Elevated work needs human verification of fall prevention and rescue controls before work proceeds.

controlRecommendationRules.title: Confirm LOTO or energized-work controls
controlRecommendationRules.recommendedAction: Confirm LOTO steps, zero-energy verification, qualified-worker assignment, and barricades before energized or electrical work proceeds.
controlRecommendationRules.verificationRequired: Verify de-energization/LOTO records, test-before-touch steps, qualified worker status, and arc-flash boundary controls.
controlRecommendationRules.humanApprovalReason: Energy-control work needs qualified human review before affected work proceeds.

controlRecommendationRules.title: Set equipment movement separation controls
controlRecommendationRules.recommendedAction: Require a spotter or barricaded exclusion zone, defined route, and pedestrian separation before equipment movement affects workers or shared access.
controlRecommendationRules.verificationRequired: Verify spotter role, communication method, barricades, route control, and pedestrian separation.
controlRecommendationRules.humanApprovalReason: Equipment movement near people needs supervisor verification of separation controls before work proceeds.

controlRecommendationRules.title: Send weather risk alert and verify thresholds
controlRecommendationRules.recommendedAction: Send a weather alert to affected supervisors and verify wind, lightning, heat, rain, or storm thresholds against the planned work before start.
controlRecommendationRules.verificationRequired: Confirm weather threshold, affected crews, task pause criteria, hydration/shelter needs, and communication path.
controlRecommendationRules.humanApprovalReason: Weather-sensitive high-risk work needs human review against jobsite thresholds before work proceeds.

rules: For high or critical risk, first recommend separating people from the highest-risk exposure or pausing affected work for review.
For high-risk work, consider lower-risk method, sequence, material, or equipment changes before relying on paperwork or PPE.
When controls or critical controls drive the risk, verify physical controls before work continues.
Training gaps must recommend task-specific training confirmation before assignment.
Permit gaps must recommend resolving permit coverage before releasing the work.
Repeated or converging patterns must recommend a focused field verification.
PPE is always treated as the final layer, not the primary control.
Critical risk adds competent-person or safety-manager review as urgent.

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Fall protection and elevated work
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Falls are high-consequence exposures; prevention, rescue readiness, and dropped-object controls need field verification.
verificationQuestions: What prevents a worker from reaching the fall edge?
Who verified anchorage, scaffold, ladder, or MEWP condition today?
How would rescue be performed if a fall occurs?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Excavation, trenching, and ground disturbance
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Excavation can change quickly; cave-in, utility strike, water, access, and atmospheric conditions require competent-person review.
verificationQuestions: Has the competent person inspected the excavation today?
What protective system is being used and does it match the soil/depth condition?
Are utilities located, exposed, isolated, or otherwise controlled?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Electrical energy and LOTO
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Energy-control failures can create fatal shock, arc-flash, or unexpected-startup exposure.
permitOrPlanTriggers: energized electrical permit or task authorization
verificationQuestions: Can the work be de-energized before proceeding?
Who verified zero energy and how was it documented?
Are qualified-worker status, boundaries, and PPE matched to the task?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Hot work and fire prevention
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Spark-producing work can ignite hidden or nearby combustibles; controls must be verified at the work face.
verificationQuestions: What combustibles are inside the spark path or adjacent spaces?
Who is assigned as fire watch and when does post-work monitoring end?
Where is the extinguisher and is it appropriate for the exposure?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Mobile equipment and line-of-fire exposure
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Equipment and people sharing space creates struck-by and caught-between exposure unless separation is actively controlled.
permitOrPlanTriggers: lift or movement plan when applicable
verificationQuestions: How are workers physically separated from the equipment route?
Who is the spotter and what communication method is being used?
Are backing, blind spots, public access, and shared routes controlled?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Crane, rigging, and suspended loads
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Lifting operations can expose crews to dropped loads, line-of-fire, equipment instability, and weather-sensitive conditions.
verificationQuestions: Has the lift plan been reviewed against load, radius, ground, and weather conditions?
Who is the qualified rigger or signal person?
How is the suspended-load exclusion zone controlled?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Weather-sensitive work
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Weather can change the risk profile for elevated work, lifting, excavation, electrical work, hot work, and heat exposure.
permitOrPlanTriggers: heat illness prevention plan when applicable
verificationQuestions: What weather threshold stops or changes the task?
Who is monitoring changes and how will crews be notified?
Does weather affect access, ground stability, electrical exposure, or heat stress?

domainUnderstandingRules.label: Confined space and atmospheric hazards
domainUnderstandingRules.whyItMatters: Confined spaces can create atmospheric, engulfment, configuration, and rescue hazards that require strict pre-entry controls.
verificationQuestions: Is this a permit-required confined space?
What atmospheric testing and ventilation are documented?
Who is the attendant and what rescue method is ready?

gusCoachGuardrails.systemRole: Gus is a calm mentor and AI Safety Coach. Gus teaches with a Field Coach method: notice what is happening, explain why it matters, ask one practical field question, then give the next safe step.
authorityBoundaries: Gus is not the competent person, supervisor, engineer, qualified person, or legal advisor.
Gus uses verified platform rules, company rules, and provided jobsite context first.
If information is missing, Gus asks for it.
If a safety-critical item is unknown, Gus marks the plan incomplete.
Gus gives practical planning help and draft recommendations only.

forbiddenOutputRewrites.rewriteTo: aligned with available platform checks and pending human review
not ready to start until reviewed by a qualified human

doNotClaim: Do not approve permits or release work.
Do not declare final OSHA compliance.
Do not claim work is safe, cleared, or guaranteed.
Do not replace safety-manager, supervisor, or competent-person judgment.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Missing controls raise risk
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Risk level moderate/high/critical and top drivers include controls.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Fatality potential plus missing controls forces critical
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Risk level critical, score at least 81, escalation required, stop-work review recommended.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Missing data lowers confidence
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Confidence low and missing data lists jobsite, task, trade, and control effectiveness.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Imminent danger cannot be downgraded by low numeric scores
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Risk level critical and score at least 81.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: High and critical findings require escalation
Readiness gap on high-risk task keeps score inside raised band
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Missing permit on hot work raises risk to high with score 61-80.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Recommendations follow hierarchy of controls
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: First six recommendation control types are elimination, substitution, engineering, administrative, ppe, competent_person_review.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Scores stay normalized
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Output score always remains 0-100 even when explicit inputs are out of range.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Explanation uses guarded wording
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Explanation says based on available data, potential risk, and review recommended.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Critical-control gap escalates high-consequence work
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Trench entry without competent-person review becomes critical with immediate action timeframe.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Converging signals raise likelihood
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Multiple modules pointing to fall exposure produce high/critical risk and a converging-signals driver.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Strong documented controls avoid false critical-control gap
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Fall-protection work with guardrail, rescue plan, and competent-person inspection has no critical-control gap.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: Domain understanding recognizes evidence
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Fall protection evidence produces permit/plan focus and field verification questions.

validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.name: No recognized safety discipline is conservative
validationScenariosThatAlreadyWork.expected: Concept list empty, confidence low, missing information asks for more task, hazard, and control evidence.

recommendedFreshProjectBuildOrder: Start with the input and output contracts.
Implement deterministic scoring and risk band classification.
Add hazard profile matching and missing-data detection.
Add escalation overrides for imminent danger, fatality potential, critical controls, and readiness gaps.
Add control recommendation rules and evidence references.
Add plain-language score explanations and missing-information output.
Add Gus as a draft-only coach after the rules engine is stable.
Add golden tests for every validation scenario before adding UI.
Only then connect database, auth, dashboards, and LLM providers.
